title: Dog Paw Pad Burned and Peeling: First Aid and Treatment Guide
meta_desc: Learn how to treat a dog's burned or peeling paw pads. Find out if it's an emergency and how to provide immediate care for your pet's recovery.
content_html:
What is a Paw Pad Burn?

A paw pad burn occurs when the sensitive skin on the underside of your dog's feet is damaged by extreme heat, such as hot pavement, or caustic chemicals. When the pad begins to peel, it typically indicates a second-degree burn where the outer protective layer has separated from the sensitive tissue underneath, leaving the area raw and painful.

Urgency Level: Medium

We understand how distressing it is to see your pet in pain. This condition is classified as a medium urgency level. While it is rarely immediately life-threatening, it is highly painful and carries a high risk of infection. You should contact a veterinarian within 24 hours for a professional assessment, pain management, and appropriate bandaging.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing a clear, high-resolution photo of the affected paw to your veterinary team can be incredibly helpful. It allows the professional to assess the depth of the burn and look for signs of infection or foreign debris. This visual information helps them determine if your dog needs to be squeezed in for an immediate appointment or if there are specific first-aid steps you should take before arriving at the clinic.

faqs.question: Should I peel off the loose skin?
faqs.answer: No, you should never pull or peel the skin yourself as this can cause further pain and damage. Let a veterinarian trim away any necrotic tissue safely.

faqs.question: Can I put Neosporin on the burn?
faqs.answer: Avoid using human ointments unless specifically directed by your vet, as dogs often lick the area and some ingredients can be harmful if ingested.

faqs.question: How can I prevent paw burns in the future?
faqs.answer: Always test the pavement with the back of your hand for seven seconds; if it is too hot for you, it is too hot for your dog's paws.
